description: An ERP and CAM platform for professional instrument makers. Combines design tools, CNC machining workflows, and business operations into one cohesive system built for luthiers.

typography.family-mono: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, 'SF Mono', Menlo, Consolas, monospace

js_features: feature tab switcher on features page

pages.name: Home
pages.description: Landing page that establishes The Production Shop as the all-in-one platform for professional luthiers. Communicates the value prop clearly: design tools + CAM + business operations in one place. Tone is professional but craft-forward â€” this is a tool built by a luthier, for luthiers.
sections: Hero: bold headline, subhead, two CTAs (Get Started, Watch Demo). Background has a subtle dark-wood grain texture via CSS gradient.
Social proof bar: logos or text callouts â€” 'Used in production shops across the US'
Feature overview: 3-column icon cards â€” Design Studio, CAM Pipeline, Business Tools
How it works: numbered 3-step flow â€” Design â†’ Machine â†’ Ship
Testimonial: one featured quote from a guitar builder
CTA banner: full-width dark section, 'Start building smarter' with email signup input
Footer: logo, nav links, copyright

pages.name: Features
pages.description: Deep-dive into the three main module domains. Uses a tab or accordion layout to show Design, CAM, and Business features separately. Each feature has an icon, title, and 2-sentence description.
sections: Page header: 'Everything a production shop needs'
Tab switcher: Design | CAM | Production | Business
Design tab: Guitar Dimensions, Scale Length Designer, Rosette Designer, Art Studio, Blueprint Importer
CAM tab: Quick Cut, Adaptive Lab, Saw Lab, Bridge Lab, Drilling Lab, G-code Explainer
Production tab: RMOS Manufacturing Candidates, Live Monitor, CNC History, Compare Runs
Business tab: Instrument Costing, CNC ROI Calculator, Cash Flow Planner, Engineering Estimator
Footer

pages.name: Pricing
pages.description: Simple, honest pricing page. Single plan or tiered options. Emphasize value for solo luthiers and small shops. Include a FAQ section below the pricing cards.
sections: Page header: 'Simple pricing for serious builders'

Pricing cards: Free (explore tools, no CNC export), Pro ($49/mo â€” full CAM pipeline, unlimited exports, RMOS), Shop ($149/mo â€” multi-user, priority support, custom post-processors). Highlight Pro as recommended.
Feature comparison table: rows are key features, columns are tiers
FAQ: 6 questions â€” What is a post-processor? Can I cancel anytime? Does it work with my CNC brand? Is my data secure? Can I export G-code without a subscription? Do you offer a student discount?
Bottom CTA: 'Start your free trial â€” no credit card required'
Footer

pages.name: About
pages.description: The story behind The Production Shop. Written from the perspective of a licensed engineer and luthier who built this to solve real problems in his own shop. Human, specific, credible.
sections: Page header: 'Built by a luthier, engineered for production'
Story section: The founder's background â€” P.E. license, CNC machining, guitar building, the gap he saw between traditional craft and modern manufacturing tools
Mission: one clear paragraph on what The Production Shop exists to do
Technology: brief callout â€” Vue 3, Python backend, EPANET-grade engineering precision applied to lutherie
Values: 3 values â€” Craft First, Engineering Precision, Builder Community
Contact teaser: 'Have a question or want to talk shop?'
Footer

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Simple contact page with a form and direct contact info. Professional but approachable.
sections: Page header: 'Get in touch'

Two-column layout: left column has contact form (Name, Email, Subject dropdown: General / Support / Partnership / Feature Request, Message, Submit button). Right column has direct info: email, response time promise, links to docs and community.
Below form: 'Looking for documentation?' with link
Footer
